violations.title: Missing Image Alt Text
violations.description: Images must have alternate text to convey their purpose to screen reader users.
violations.explanation: All images must have an alt attribute that describes the image content. Screen readers use this text to convey the image's purpose to visually impaired users. Decorative images should use alt="".

violations.title: Insufficient Color Contrast
violations.description: Text must have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text.
violations.explanation: Text must have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text (18pt or 14pt bold) against its background. This ensures readability for users with low vision or color blindness.

violations.title: HTML Element Missing Language Attribute
violations.description: The <html> element must have a lang attribute specifying the page language.
violations.explanation: The <html> element must have a lang attribute specifying the page language. Screen readers use this to load the correct pronunciation rules.

violations.title: Form Input Missing Label
violations.description: Every form input must have a programmatically associated label.
violations.explanation: Every form input must have a programmatically associated label using <label for="">, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. This tells screen reader users what information to enter.

fixSnippets.fixed:
<label for="search-input">Search services</label>
<input type="text" id="search-input" placeholder="Search services...">

violations.title: Page Missing Main Landmark
violations.description: Each page should have exactly one main landmark to identify the primary content.
violations.explanation: Each page should have exactly one <main> element (or role="main") to identify the primary content area. This allows screen reader users to skip directly to the main content.

violations.title: Heading Levels Out of Order
violations.description: Headings must follow a logical, sequential order without skipping levels.
violations.explanation: Headings must follow a logical, sequential order (h1 → h2 → h3). Skipping heading levels (e.g., h1 → h3) creates navigation confusion for screen reader users.
